prompt: The travel desk is reconciling two itineraries before the approval queue closes. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local travel-desk executable for all record access; do not inspect or query its SQL seed or runtime directory directly. Find the trip named “Kyoto Research Symposium Trip” at “Kyoto” and the trip named “Montreal Supplier Site Visit” at “Montreal”. The executable is `python3 travel_desk.py`: use `search --name NAME --location LOCATION` for a name-and-location search and `get --id ID` for a full record. Your first travel-data shell execution must start both independent search processes concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. After both results return, retrieve a branch only when its search produced exactly one stable ID, using only that returned ID. When both branches resolve, your next travel-data shell execution must start both independent get processes concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. Base the brief on full get records, not search summaries. This is a read-only audit: do not use list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write `brief.txt` with exactly three single-line sentences and no other text. The first two lines, in Kyoto-then-Montreal order, must have the exact shape `<name> at <location> has status <status>.` If both branches resolve, line three must be `Their statuses match: <status>.` when equal or `Their statuses differ: <first-status> versus <second-status>.` when unequal. Preserve status values exactly as returned.
